seo.title: Falls Prevention for Hospital Systems | Able Care
seo.description: Objective falls screening across the hospital care continuum. Capture a baseline in primary care and orthopedics, inform discharge planning, and reduce falls — and the quality penalties they trigger.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Clinician reviewing Able Assess results with a patient on a tablet in a hospital setting
blocks.headline: Falls prevention across the hospital care continuum.
blocks.subtitle: A baseline reading on senior patients before they reach acute care. Better-informed discharge planning. Reduced falls across primary care, orthopedics, inpatient and post-acute settings — lifting quality ratings and the reimbursement they unlock.

blocks.heading: What Able Assess Offers Hospital Systems

metrics.name: Outpatient baseline
metrics.whatItMeasures: Capture an objective falls-risk baseline at the Annual Wellness Visit or orthopedic outpatient appointment — before a fall sends the patient into acute care.

metrics.name: Acute care planning
metrics.whatItMeasures: Equip inpatient teams with objective functional data to inform discharge: home, post-acute setting, or home with home care to prevent a post-discharge fall.

metrics.name: Post-acute discharge confidence
metrics.whatItMeasures: Decide which post-acute location each patient should be discharged to — informed by the same standardized screen the SNF, IRF or HHA will use on intake.

blocks.heading: How it works across the care continuum

steps.title: Capture
steps.description: PCP or orthopedic outpatient staff run a five-minute objective screen — covering Medicare Advantage requirements for PT referral and informing surgical candidacy.

steps.title: Inform
steps.description: When the patient reaches inpatient care, the baseline informs the team's understanding of impairment — especially powerful when pre-intake data is on record.

steps.title: Plan
steps.description: Use the data to plan discharge: home, post-acute setting, or home with supplementary home care.

steps.title: Track
steps.description: Re-screen across the continuum. Falls outcomes ladder into the quality ratings that drive CMS reimbursement.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.whatItMeasures: Full body strength proxy. Can be captured on virtually any patient — from healthy outpatients to acute inpatients.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower-limb power and balance. Informs surgical candidacy, prehab decisions and discharge readiness.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: The 'sixth vital sign.' Linked to rehospitalization risk, length of stay and value-based care outcomes.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whatItMeasures: Falls risk in under a minute. Validated across outpatient, inpatient and post-acute settings.

blocks.heading: The numbers that matter to hospital systems

stats.label: Bed midmarket systems we're built for
Clinical certifications required to run it

testimonials.quote: Having a pre-intake baseline changed how our acute teams think about discharge. We catch the patients we used to send home and then readmit.
Grip strength was the one measure our nurses could capture on the most impaired patients. That alone made the data usable across the whole continuum.

faqs.question: Where do we deploy this across the system?
faqs.answer: We focus on three use modes: an outpatient baseline in Primary Care and Orthopedics, acute care planning for inpatients, and discharge decisions for patients going into post-acute care (SNF, IRF, HHA or home care). Many systems start with one and expand.

faqs.question: Our clinical staff don't have time for another tool.
faqs.answer: Able Assess can be run by any staff — including assistants and front-desk staff. The five-minute protocol fits within an Annual Wellness Visit, an outpatient appointment or a nursing intake. Adoption works best when mandated from the top down.

faqs.question: What about patients who are too acute or impaired?
faqs.answer: Grip strength can be taken on virtually anyone — seated or supine if needed — making it a powerful indicator across the entire care continuum, from healthy outpatients to acute inpatients.

faqs.question: How does it support our Annual Wellness Visit workflow?
faqs.answer: Objective falls screening isn't part of the AWV by default, so PCPs rely on patient self-report. Many Medicare Advantage plans won't allow PT referrals without a self-report or objective screen on record. Able Assess provides the objective screen — and the referral pathway that follows.

faqs.question: How does it integrate with our EHR?
faqs.answer: Able Assess is integration-ready with the major EHR platforms. The clinical team can walk you through the setup during a demo.

faqs.question: Can we run a pilot?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our current hospital strategy centers on partnered pilots — tracking the impact of an Able Assess rollout on falls reduction. Book a demo to scope a pilot for your system.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess across your care continuum.
blocks.bodyText: Book a walkthrough with a clinician who has piloted Able Assess inside hospital systems.
